properties.descricao: Officials say two people were killed in Russia's Bryansk, while a woman was killed in Ukraine's Sumy.

properties.titulo_evento: US-Iran ceasefire? Not for Indian sailors being killed in Hormuz
properties.descricao: The United States military has struck three vessels in the Strait of Hormuz this week, killing three Indian sailors.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war day 105: Trump halts attacks after Kharg Island threat
properties.descricao: Trump cancels planned Iran attacks, saying talks are close to a result as Tehran reviews a proposed US deal.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran weighs proposed deal but remains wary of US intentions
properties.descricao: Iran says a proposed agreement is under review by its highest leadership and could be closer than ever before.

properties.titulo_evento: South Korea’s ex-President Yoon gets 30 years over drone operation
properties.descricao: Seoul court sentences former leader for sending military drones into North Korea.

properties.titulo_evento: Stock markets surge as Trump calls off strikes on Iran, touts peace deal
properties.descricao: Wall Street and Asian markets rally on hopes for an end to the US-Israel war on Iran.

properties.descricao: Videos shared by Palestinian activists show powerful Israeli airstrikes across Gaza Strip overnight.

properties.titulo_evento: Flip flop or deliberate? - Unpacking Trump’s strategy on Iran
properties.descricao: The BBC's Gary O'Donoghue looks at the US president's mixed messaging over the war and the questions it raises.

properties.titulo_evento: Artan to referee Uefa Super Cup after losing World Cup role
properties.descricao: Somali referee Omar Artan is appointed to take charge of the Uefa Super Cup match between Paris St-Germain and Aston Villa later this summer after being denied a spot at the World Cup.

properties.titulo_evento: More than 50 Iranian military bases damaged in US strikes since start of war, satellite images show
properties.descricao: Bases across Iran have been hit by US strikes, with experts identifying damage to jets and warships.

properties.titulo_evento: Israeli air campaign intensifies across southern Lebanon

properties.titulo_evento: Mexico beats South Africa and Czechia loses to South Korea in World Cup opening matches
properties.descricao: Julián Quiñones scored the opening goal of the tournament after nine minutes and 35-year-old striker Raúl Jiménez headed in Mexico's second goal in a 2-0 opening game against South Africa that produced three red cards.

properties.titulo_evento: US slaps sanctions on Cuba's oil and gas company, accusing it of weaponising energy
properties.descricao: Cuba is already struggling under a decades-old embargo and a lack of petroleum as the US keeps pushing for a change in its economic and political model.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran says no decision made on peace deal with US after Trump touts 'great settlement'
properties.descricao: Trump said on Thursday that the two sides had reached a "great settlement" over the war and that it could be finalised over the next few days.

properties.titulo_evento: US strikes another tanker in Hormuz as it tightens Iranian port blockade
properties.descricao: CENTCOM disabled another tanker attempting to bypass the Iran blockade in a third strike this week, just hours after three Indian crew members were confirmed dead in Wednesday's strike on the MT Settebello.

properties.titulo_evento: US will seize and control Iran's Kharg Island and other key oil facilities, Trump says
properties.descricao: Trump's social media post comes after the US and Iran traded strikes for a third day, pushing the region closer to the resumption of a full-scale war.

properties.titulo_evento: Funeral for former French first lady Bernadette Chirac to take place in Paris
properties.descricao: The funeral of Bernadette Chirac, widow of former president Jacques Chirac, will take place on Friday at the Basilica of Sainte-Clotilde in Paris. Chirac died on June 5 at the age of 93.

properties.titulo_evento: World Cup 2026: Mexico beat South Africa in the opening match
properties.descricao:
The 2026 World Cup began in Mexico City with an opening ceremony filled with colour, dancing and a performance by global music star Shakira.
 
Julian Quinones and Premier League player Raul Jimenez scored the first goals of the tournament.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran's football team trains in Mexico as World Cup campaign begins

properties.titulo_evento: Trump says Iran peace deal is near after threatening new strikes
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he had halted plans for new military strikes on Iran, claiming negotiators were close to extending a fragile ceasefire. Hours before, he threatened to intensify the conflict by targeting Iran’s oil industry.

properties.titulo_evento: South Korea: Ex-President Yoon gets 30 years for sending drones into North Korea
properties.descricao: The verdict marks the latest legal blow for South Korea's impeached former leader Yoon Suk Yeol, who has already been sentenced to life in prison earlier this year.

properties.titulo_evento: Egypt's delicate balancing act in the Iran war
properties.descricao: Cairo faces criticism from its Gulf allies that support against Iran has been lacking. But Egypt's priority is to shield its fragile economy from the fallout of a widening conflict.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump claims Iran deal close, Tehran says nothing finalized
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump cancelled threatened strikes and said a deal could be signed as soon as the weekend. Tehran, on the other hand, said "it has not reached a final conclusion."

properties.titulo_evento: Iran: From clerical rule to military capture
properties.descricao: The Iran war and the country's change of leadership are reshaping Iran's power structure. Analysts say the Revolutionary Guard is emerging as the dominant force, weakening the system's clerical foundations.

properties.titulo_evento: UK: Defense Secretary John Healey quits in military spending dispute
properties.descricao: Britain's defense minister has abruptly quit his post, saying the government's plans to bolster defense spending in the coming years are inadequate. The move puts additional pressure on embattled UK PM Keir Starmer.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine: Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant's power cut off
properties.descricao: An attack near Europe's largest nuclear power plant in Russian-occupied Ukraine has caused an outage in the area. Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone attack has sparked a fire at a refinery in southern Russia.

properties.titulo_evento: Hungary makes major deal with Ukraine, its former enemy
properties.descricao: Hungarian Prime Minister Magyar announced an agreement with Kyiv about rights for a Hungarian minority in Ukraine. It paves the way for Ukraine to start EU accession talks — unthinkable under Hungary's last PM Orban.

properties.titulo_evento: After Trump threatened to attack Iran, a scramble to stop him
properties.descricao: Senior officials from Qatar, the UAE and Pakistan rang Trump as he was threatening to strike Iran "very hard tonight." Their assurance of a deal in reach convinced him to pull back.
